sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nice are struggling, having lost three of their last five matches, including a 2-1 defeat to Metz and a 1-3 loss to SC Freiburg. In contrast, Olympique de Marseille are in strong form, securing wins in three of their last five games, with a notable 3-0 victory over Stade Brestois, though they suffered a late 2-2 draw against Angers.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Recent head-to-head encounters between Nice and Marseille have been evenly split, with each team winning one of the last two matches 2-0. Nice won at home in January 2025, while Marseille triumphed at their ground in September 2024, indicating a competitive rivalry.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Marseille sit second in Ligue 1 with 25 points and an impressive 28 goals scored, showcasing their attacking prowess led by Mason Greenwood (8 goals). Nice, positioned 9th with 17 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 18 goals and underperforming their expected goals by nearly 3.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Nice will miss midfielder Hicham Boudaoui, potentially weakening their midfield, while Sofiane Diop (6 goals) remains a key threat. Marseille's attack is bolstered by Greenwood and Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, though they are without defender Amir Murillo, which could test their backline.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Nice tend to score early, with a strong record of opening the scoring in recent matches, but their defensive frailties could be exposed by Marseille's late-game potency (10 goals after the 76th minute). Marseille's higher possession and final third entries suggest they may dominate play.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Marseille's superior form, league standing, and attacking strength, coupled with Sofascore's 56% probability of an away win, they are favorites to take the three points. Nice's home advantage and past success against Marseille might keep it close, but a narrow victory for the visitors seems likely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Olympique de Marseille lead Nice 2-0 at the Allianz Riviera. Marseille have been clinical in their approach, capitalizing on their opportunities with goals from Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang and Mason Greenwood in the first half. Nice, despite controlling a significant portion of the game, have struggled to convert their chances into goals, leaving them with a mountain to climb in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Nice have shown dominance in possession and territorial play, frequently entering the final third and penalty area. However, their inability to finish big chances has been a glaring issue. Players like Terem Moffi and Mohamed-Ali Cho have been active in attack, but they’ve lacked the cutting edge needed to break through Marseille’s defense. Defensively, Nice have held their own in aerial duels, but lapses in concentration allowed Marseille to score twice.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Olympique de Marseille have been efficient and ruthless on the counter. Despite having less possession, they’ve created more dangerous opportunities and converted two of them into goals. Aubameyang and Greenwood have been pivotal in attack, showcasing sharpness in front of goal. Defensively, Marseille have been solid, though they’ve struggled in aerial battles. Their goalkeeper has also played a key role in keeping Nice at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Marseille started with a strong attacking spell early on, quickly gaining the upper hand. Nice responded with sustained pressure in the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent in multiple phases. However, Marseille regained control at crucial moments, particularly around their goals, showing their ability to strike decisively. Towards the end of the half, Nice mounted another wave of attacks, but Marseille’s defense held firm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Nice are likely to come out with renewed vigor in the second half, desperate to reduce the deficit. With home support behind them, they’ll push for an early goal to shift the momentum. Marseille, on the other hand, will probably adopt a more cautious approach, looking to protect their lead while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. The game could open up if Nice score early, but Marseille’s clinical finishing might prove decisive if they maintain their composure.
